conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Artem is Head of Supply Chain with 5+ years of e-commerce experience, most recently at Jumpzylla LLC (a US-based trampoline brand selling on Amazon). He was let go in late March 2026 following a stockout incident and is now in active job search mode targeting senior SC / Director-level remote roles. In parallel, he is building a course business on Udemy and managing consulting-oriented work.

**Personal context**

Artem is based in Kyiv (UTC+3) and communicates in Ukrainian day-to-day but produces professional and LinkedIn content in English for international reach. He has a partner, and they have a Shar-Pei dog. He has expressed interest in international relocation, with Spain (particularly Valencia) identified as a preferred destination. He enjoys chess, FC 26 on Xbox, and poker.

**Top of mind**

Artem is in active job search mode with a May 1 deadline, simultaneously running LinkedIn outreach (twice-weekly posts), applying to companies including PawChamp/SKELAR, and nurturing warm contacts (Gavin, Kevin at SmartWarehousing, Mark Sherman at Green Wave, Josh at Spreetail). He is launching his *Fundamentals of E-Commerce Supply Chain* course on Udemy with personal sales and reviews as the immediate goal, and is developing a "Job Loss" (fired SC mistakes) course series in parallel. He is also tracking health indicators and has recently begun vitamin D3 supplementation (4000 IU with K2-MK7) following lab results showing critically low levels, and is awaiting an ENT consultation based on ear swab findings.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

Artem built an extensive job search and personal brand infrastructure: multiple LinkedIn posts (personal intro, talent vs. gap, seasonal inventory, McKinsey Forward acceptance), cover letters for numerous companies, and a refined resume/LinkedIn profile positioning him as a results-driven SC leader (not an executor). He was accepted into McKinsey Forward during this period. His LinkedIn About section opening line was deliberately revised to "I build supply chains that reduce costs, scale operations, and grow teams" to avoid contradicting his departure circumstances. His tagline settled on "Optimizing processes. Cutting costs. Building teams."

He developed course content across two tracks: (1) *Fundamentals of E-Commerce Supply Chain* (8 modules, 16 lessons, nearly fully recorded) as the entry product in a funnel, and (2) a "Fired" series of standalone ~1-hour courses on SC career-ending mistakes, with a consistent format: real case study → mistake breakdown → what should have been done → practical checklist. Course 4 in the "Fired" series (hiding problems from leadership) includes a three-layer structure covering psychology, failure mechanics, and a trigger/early-warning prevention system. A note is saved to add seasonal trigger logic to Lesson 3 of the B-14FT case course (full cycle calculated backward from season start date).

For Jumpzylla's operational wind-down period, Artem produced a 2026 KPI planning document covering his team: **Katarina Nikolić** (Logistics Manager — freight, last mile, Sellerboard accuracy, container invoice reduction, tax refund calculation, COGS dashboard, spare parts process, packing rulebook) and **Claudia Afas** (Inventory Manager — inventory snapshot reporting, revised in-transit forecast, reorder point definitions, shipping plan and order creation for blended containers). He also built a supply chain action plan, meeting agendas, and execution documents for team syncs, working across Ukrainian and English depending on audience. Key internal stakeholders included **Oxana** (COO), **Adrian** (senior leader running weekly 1:1s), and **Anna from DPW** (customs).

3PL evaluation work included a detailed head-to-head of Extensiv vs. Cin7 Omni (Extensiv recommended at 88/100 for Jumpzylla's FBM + multi-warehouse + two-box SKU profile), a Spreetail partnership negotiation with contact **Josh** (Tier 4 offered with path to Tier 5; non-compete declined; SLA and storage terms partially aligned), and a Smart Ship Network contract risk analysis. Artem also built a FedEx/UPS rate inquiry process for Green Wave Electronics partnership evaluation.

**3PL Reference — Smart Ship Network LLC ("Smart Shipped"):**
Warehouses: Victorville CA ($13.50/pallet, $27 oversized), Haslet TX ($11/$22), Wind Gap PA ($13/$26). Inbound month free. Container unload $590, pallet in/out $10, parcel: $1.75 (<10 lb) / $2.35 (10–100 lb) / $4.70 (101–200 lb), labeling $0.45, order processing $8, carton pick $1.50. Net-15 invoices, 1.5%/mo late fee. Annual auto-increase: max(4%, ECI). FedEx: 15% upcharge included, excludes Remote Area surcharge.

**Active product models (3PL):** 8FT, 10FT, N-12FT-183, N-14FT (single-box units); 15FT and 16FT are each one unit shipped as two separate boxes (Box 1 + Box 2).

Artem's Code of Honor (10 rules) was formalized, covering principles such as "know, don't just be confident," inventory control first, no manual data entry, ownership over management, and delegating as the path to growing value.

*Earlier context*

Artem was building out Jumpzylla's COGS reduction roadmap with quantified savings targets: 3PL zone reduction ($270K–$370K/year), container delivery cost reduction ($120K–$150K/year), box optimization ($15K–$20K/year); total $618K–$916K annually excluding manufacturing reductions and a potential ~$500K one-time tax refund. Manufacturing cost targets: 15FT at $101.20 (from $114), 16FT at $115 (from $130). He also explored consulting fee structures for performance-based SC optimization engagements and researched the Udemy Amazon FBA course market, identifying a gap for practical e-commerce-specific SC content.

Artem explored Spain relocation in depth — Digital Nomad Visa identified as best fit given contractor status, with Valencia as top city recommendation; Beckham Law (24% flat tax) flagged as a priority with a six-month application deadline post-TIE card. He was also researching extended family stays in Phuket (7–8 months) and international pet travel requirements for the Shar-Pei.

*Long-term background*

Artem has been developing e-commerce supply chain educational content since at least mid-2025, building a course ecosystem targeting SC assistants through director-level professionals, with an emphasis on execution-focused, practical materials (checklists, homework, real case studies). He created foundational course artifacts including a supply chain glossary, SC Manager and SC Officer responsibility documents, a 9-course ecosystem organized into four clusters (Sourcing & China, Cost Cutting, Fulfillment, Management Level), and lesson scripts including a 59-slide QC lesson. His long-term income model targets combining a senior SC role with Udemy course revenue. He has experience with China sourcing, ocean and air freight, Amazon FBA/FBM, 3PL management, COGS optimization, and inventory management, and has taught himself to frame this expertise as differentiated educational content for emerging-market professionals seeking remote e-commerce careers.

project_memories.019b0872-a57b-775f-88d2-581a0753635a:
**Purpose & context**

Artem runs a business selling oversized/large-format products and is currently evaluating 3PL (third-party logistics) partnerships. A key focus is ensuring logistics providers can handle the specific demands of oversized inventory — including Amazon Prime eligibility, SLA compliance, peak-period scalability, and responsive communication.

Artem communicates in Ukrainian but frequently needs deliverables in English, particularly for professional business contexts.

**Current state**

Artem is in the onboarding/due diligence phase with a 3PL provider called Smartshipped (contact: Sam). He has been conducting reference checks with contacts provided by Smartshipped, focused on their performance with oversized product catalogues specifically.

**Approach & patterns**

- Prefers concise, professional business writing — avoids unnecessary filler or redundant questions
- Gives direct, specific revision instructions and knows what he wants removed or adjusted
- Works across languages (Ukrainian internally, English for external/professional deliverables)
project_memories.0199bb45-80aa-724e-9237-a04a58c3f417:
**Purpose & context**

Artem is building a comprehensive e-commerce supply chain educational program. The core goal is to create professional, practical courses that help students — ranging from beginners (students, career changers, Amazon sellers) to more experienced practitioners — apply supply chain knowledge directly to real business operations.

The course content is deeply rooted in Amazon FBA/e-commerce operations: procurement cycles, supplier vetting, quality control, shipping logistics, customs, landed cost analysis, fulfillment models, and inventory management. Artem has demonstrated strong domain expertise in these areas, consistently requesting technically accurate, data-driven content with real-world calculations and examples.

The broader vision includes four courses targeting different experience levels, with the introductory mini-course as the current focus.

**Current state**

- Video content has been recorded; Artem is now in the editing and post-production phase
- A technical brief for a video editor was developed (in Ukrainian), simplified to focus on essential editorial instructions, visual style, and delivery workflow — no complex technical specs
- Visual style is modeled on Amazon's aesthetic (orange, dark navy, white; clean sans-serif fonts like Inter), adapted to avoid trademark issues
- Course layout: instructor on one-third of screen, slides on two-thirds, edited in Canva
- Lesson scripts (Lessons 1–15) have been developed in English, with paired Canva presentation manuals (max 9 slides per lesson, 8–10 minute video format)
- A video editor is involved; Artem is the course creator and decision-maker

**On the horizon**

- Lesson 16 is planned as a wrap-up covering critical mistakes and a master checklist
- Potential expansion into automation tutorials for supply chain tasks (using Claude as a processing engine with Excel/spreadsheet data)
- Broader course series for more advanced audiences is in the pipeline

**Key learnings & principles**

- Content should be practical and immediately applicable — specific formulas, comparison tables, step-by-step calculations, and real cost examples resonate strongly (e.g., how one centimeter in packaging affects annual FBA fees)
- Mentor tone works best: ~80% coach, 20% experienced colleague; simple English suitable for B1–B2 level speakers
- Documents and briefs should be concise and jargon-free — Artem prefers simplified, enforceable instructions over comprehensive but impractical specs
- Slide design should be minimal; flashy animations distract from educational content
- Amazon-inspired visual language communicates e-commerce professionalism effectively without requiring official branding

**Approach & patterns**

- Artem works lesson-by-lesson through a pre-defined course roadmap, approving structure before requesting continuation
- Each lesson follows a consistent format: full video script with timecodes + instructor notes + Canva design manual
- Prefers to receive both a comprehensive version and a simplified/practical version of documents, then selects or refines
- Communicates in Ukrainian or Russian; course content is produced in English
- References external examples (YouTube videos, competitor courses) to guide style decisions

**Tools & resources**

- Canva: primary tool for slide creation and video editing/assembly
- Excel/spreadsheets (e.g., SoStocked inventory templates): used for supply chain automation examples
- Amazon Seller Central ecosystem: FBA, AWD, FNSKU, fee structures — central reference point for course content
- Claude: used for scripting, document creation, financial modeling, and spreadsheet analysis
